text.title: HAProxy Production Core Rules
text.summary: 6 testable rules for production-grade HAProxy. Each rule pairs a binary check + sourced rationale.

rule.statement: TLS frontends MUST bind with `ssl crt /path/to/cert.pem alpn h2,http/1.1` and set `ssl-default-bind-options no-sslv3 no-tlsv10 no-tlsv11 prefer-client-ciphers`. Cipher list MUST exclude RC4, 3DES, EXPORT, CBC-only ciphers.

rationale.source: Mozilla TLS guidelines + HAProxy SSL/TLS docs

rule.rationale: TLS 1.0/1.1 and weak ciphers fail PCI-DSS scans and expose users to BEAST/POODLE-class attacks. ALPN advertisement enables HTTP/2 which is mandatory for modern browsers.

rule.statement: Public HTTP frontends MUST declare a `stick-table type ip size 100k expire 10m store http_req_rate(10s)` and reject IPs above the configured threshold (default: 100 req/10s) with HTTP 429.
rule.rationale: Without rate limiting, a single misbehaving client can saturate the backend pool. Stick-tables are in-process and require no external Redis — operationally cheaper than a distributed limiter for single-LB-instance topologies.

rule.statement: `global { nbthread N; cpu-map auto:1/1-N 0-(N-1) }` where N equals (or is <=) the number of physical cores on the LB host. `daemon` mode + multi-process (`nbproc`) is forbidden — use threads.
rule.rationale: `nbthread` with CPU pinning eliminates the cross-CPU cache misses that `nbproc` introduced. On a 16-core box, properly threaded HAProxy handles 2-3× more TLS connections than the same box in nbproc mode.

rule.statement: `global maxconn` MUST be set to (available RAM / ~16 KiB per connection). `defaults maxconn` MUST equal the same value or be lower. Per-frontend `maxconn` MUST be set to throttle individual frontends.
rule.rationale: Unbounded `maxconn` lets HAProxy OOM-kill itself under SYN floods. Sizing by RAM keeps the process inside its allocation and lets the kernel rather than the heap apply backpressure.

rule.statement: Backends MUST use `option httpchk GET /health` plus `http-check expect status 200` (or stricter body match). `option httpchk` without `expect` is forbidden.
rule.rationale: Without `expect`, any HTTP response (including 502 from a crashed app behind a still-listening proxy) counts as healthy. The `expect` clause is what makes the check meaningful.

rule.statement: Active-passive HAProxy deployments MUST run `keepalived` with VRRP. Master and backup MUST share the same `virtual_router_id`, distinct `priority` values, and a `track_script` that returns non-zero when HAProxy is unhealthy. Plain DNS round-robin is forbidden for HA.
rule.rationale: VRRP fails over the VIP in < 2 seconds and reroutes existing connections to the surviving node. DNS-based failover relies on client TTL honour and can take minutes.
